Package org.eventb.core.ast

Class Expression

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.ast.Formula<Expression>
org.eventb.core.ast.Expression
Direct Known Subclasses:
AssociativeExpression, AtomicExpression, BinaryExpression, BoolExpression, ExtendedExpression, Identifier, IntegerLiteral, QuantifiedExpression, SetExtension, UnaryExpression
public abstract class Expression extends Formula<Expression>
Common class for event-B expressions. TODO: document Expression.
Since:
1.0

  • Method Details

    • getType

      public final Type getType()
      Returns the type of this expression if it is type-checked, or null otherwise. Once the type of this expression is known, it will never change.
      Returns:
      the type of this expression or null
      See Also:
      • Formula.isTypeChecked()

    • isATypeExpression

      public boolean isATypeExpression()
      Returns whether this expression denotes a type.
      Returns:
      true iff this expression denotes a type.

    • toType

      public Type toType()
      Returns the type corresponding to this type-checked expression.

      The returned type is not the type of this expression.

      Returns:
      the type represented by this expression
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - when this expression doesn't denote a type or is not type-checked
      Since:
      2.0
      See Also:
      • Type.toExpression()
      • isATypeExpression()

    • typeCheck

      public final ITypeCheckResult typeCheck(ITypeEnvironment environment, Type expectedType)
      Statically type-checks this expression, whose expected type is known.

      Returns the TypeCheckResult containing all the informations about the type-check run.

      Parameters:
      environment - an initial type environment
      expectedType - expected type of this expression
      Returns:
      the result of the type checker
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if this formula is not well-formed
      IllegalArgumentException - if the given type or type environment has been built with a different formula factory
      See Also:
      • Formula.isWellFormed()
